description:
**Problem**
`DepKind::WaitsFor` is now a typed edge kind, but the edge still has no typed payload for Gas City/Go gate behavior. Current Go/Gas City sources use waits-for as a fanout gate with `all-children` / `any-children` semantics and optional spawner identity. Treating waits-for as only a simple blocker is enough for vocabulary/readiness classification, but not enough for correct gate semantics.

**Design**
Add a typed waits-for edge payload instead of storing untyped JSON metadata as canonical state. The payload should model:

- gate: `all-children` or `any-children`;
- optional spawner id;
- compatibility projection for Gas City/Go JSON where a legacy metadata string is still expected;
- validation at the CLI/adapter boundary.

The ready-work evaluator should apply the typed payload to the spawner children and keep the current simple closed-target behavior only where that is deliberately the fallback semantics.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Typed waits-for payload exists in the owning core/surface edge model
- [ ] `all-children` blocks until every relevant child is closed
- [ ] `any-children` blocks until at least one relevant child is closed
- [ ] Optional spawner id is validated and projected
- [ ] Gas City-compatible dep JSON can still expose the legacy metadata shape where needed
- [ ] Tests cover simple blocker, all-children, any-children, and missing/deleted spawner behavior
- [ ] Existing Floor 1 dep-kind tests remain green

**References**
- `docs/go-parity/primitives/dep-kinds.md`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/beads/internal/types/types.go`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/beads/internal/storage/issueops/blocked.go`
- `~/vendor/github.com/gastownhall/gascity/internal/formula/compile.go`
title: Floor 1: typed waits-for edge payload and gate semantics

description:
**Problem**
Migration integration coverage still draws fixture state from three different places: inline helpers in `cli/migration.rs`, the `fixtures/legacy_store.rs` corpus/harness, and the separate `fixtures/migration_store.rs` loader. That split makes new migration tests too easy to write in a one-off style and keeps store-ref setup logic regrowing in the test file.

**Design**
Collapse migration fixture data and store-ref helper APIs onto one canonical fixture surface. The migration tests should describe scenarios, not reimplement git/blob/meta plumbing, and dead duplicate fixture loaders should be removed or folded into the canonical path.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Migration store-ref tests use one shared fixture/harness surface instead of split local helpers
- [ ] Duplicate or dead migration fixture entry points are removed or merged
- [ ] AGENTS/doc guidance points new migration tests at the canonical fixture path

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/migration_store.rs`
- `AGENTS.md`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Closed in /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs on jj change slzrvnwzoprq / commit 7ff4d763f024. Unified migration store-ref helpers onto crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store.rs, deleted the dead crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/migration_store.rs loader, moved v0_1_26_minimal into crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/legacy_store_corpus/, and updated AGENTS/plan docs so the canonical migration fixture path stays truthful. Strong adversarial review found and I fixed two truthfulness gaps: crates/beads-rs/tests/AGENTS.md no longer points at dead fixture-data/, and the closeout plan now reflects the deleted loader + moved corpus. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration cli::migration:: -- --nocapture; cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration cli::migration:: --features slow-tests -- --nocapture (56 passed); just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo xtest (1125 passed).

title: Unify migration store fixture data and harness APIs
